mnemonics.title: RRBC - The Four Pillars of Experimental Design
mnemonics.content: Remember the four core principles using RRBC: Replication (Repeat it), Randomization (Random assignment), Blinding (Block bias), Control groups (Compare against baseline). Think of it as 'Really Reliable Because Careful' -- experiments are really reliable because you are careful about these four principles.

mnemonics.title: Single vs Double Blinding - The Eye Rule
mnemonics.content: Single-blind = one eye closed (only subjects are 'blind' to their group). Double-blind = both eyes closed (both subjects AND experimenters are 'blind'). More eyes closed means less bias can creep in. Remember: 'One eye for subjects, two eyes for everyone.'

mnemonics.title: ANOVA then Tukey - The 'Any then Which' Sequence
mnemonics.content: ANOVA answers 'Is there ANY significant difference among groups?' Tukey's HSD answers 'WHICH specific pairs are different?' Remember the sequence: A comes before T in the alphabet, just as ANOVA comes before Tukey. First ask 'Any difference?' (ANOVA), then ask 'Which ones?' (Tukey).
